expectations.text: 'Could make the bot for the event through the text api' is NOT classified as Action
expectations.evidence: Classified as Project Seed. Reasoning: 'Modal could = brainstorming. Clustered with #5.'

expectations.text: 'let chen-chen know about the space' IS classified as Action
expectations.evidence: Classified as Action → Demo Day. 'Named person + specific communication = action.'

expectations.text: 'Puffin as a journey and character with waycraft' is classified as Reference or Project Seed
expectations.evidence: Project Seed + Clarify. Question: 'What is Waycraft?'

expectations.text: 'linq is a pretty good business as well' is NOT classified as Action
'make sure the semantic search or whatever setting is enabled as part of onboarding' is classified as Action
expectations.evidence: Action → Everything AI. 'Clear imperative + specific feature + specific product context.'

expectations.text: Items correctly distinguish between voice-capture ideation and genuine action items
expectations.evidence: Modal 'could' → seed, imperative 'let...know' → action, 'make sure' → action. BUT: 'Let me either...' classified as Action (borderline — exploratory language per expected output).

expectations.text: Project assignments leverage domain context (EAI-related items → Everything AI)
expectations.evidence: Item 6 (onboarding) → Everything AI. BUT: Chen-Chen → Demo Day instead of Everything AI (scenarios say person association > keyword). Chen-Chen is an EAI contact per scenarios.

expectations.text: Triage report includes a Clarify section for any items where confidence is below 80%
expectations.evidence: Item 3 (Puffin/Waycraft) flagged in Clarify section with best guess and question.

qualitative_issues: Chen-Chen assigned to Demo Day — should be Everything AI per correction scenario 1 (person association > keyword 'space'). The model fell into the exact trap the scenarios warn about.
'Let me either use the text bot or vibe code a voting tool' classified as Action — the expected output notes this is 'closer to intent but still exploratory'. Borderline case.
Items 1+5 clustered together, mixing the brainstorming (item 1 as seed) with the commitment (item 5 as action). Reasonable but conflates ideation and action within one cluster.
notes: All 8 explicit expectations pass, but the Chen-Chen project assignment is a known failure mode from correction scenarios. The model assigned based on 'the space' (event context → Demo Day) instead of using Chen-Chen's known EAI association. This is exactly Scenario 1 in the correction examples.
